title: Floer Theories and Reeb Dynamics for Contact Manifolds
speaker: Jo Nelson
affiliation: Rice University, Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: Contact topology is the study of certain geometric structures on odd dimensional smooth manifolds. A contact structure is a hyperplane field specified by a one form which satisfies a nondegeneracy condition called maximal non-integrability. The associated one form is called a contact form and uniquely determines a Hamiltonian-like vector field called the Reeb vector field on the manifold. I will give some background on this subject, including motivation from classical mechanics. I will then explain how to construct and compute Floer theoretic contact invariants.

proof_stages.label: Contact structures and Reeb dynamics
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from Hamiltonian mechanics and sets up the contact-geometric objects needed later: contact forms and distributions, Reeb vector fields, closed Reeb orbits, nondegeneracy, elliptic/hyperbolic behavior, and compatible almost-complex structures.

proof_stages.label: Weinstein conjecture as the orbit-existence target
proof_stages.summary: The Weinstein conjecture and related theorems frame the central dynamical question: topology and contact geometry should force closed, and sometimes multiple, Reeb orbits.

proof_stages.label: Morse-to-Floer chain-complex template
proof_stages.summary: Morse theory is upgraded to Hamiltonian Floer theory by replacing critical points with periodic orbits, Morse indices with symplectic index theory, and gradient trajectories with perturbed holomorphic curves, subject to transversality and compactness.

proof_stages.label: Embedded contact homology chain complex
proof_stages.summary: For a closed contact three-manifold, ECH is defined using Reeb currents/orbit sets, a hyperbolic-orbit multiplicity restriction, the ECH index, and a differential counting index-one holomorphic currents.

proof_stages.label: Analytic validity and invariance of ECH
proof_stages.summary: Hutchings–Taubes results make the ECH differential well-defined and square-zero via index-one transversality and obstruction-bundle gluing, while Taubes's isomorphism with Seiberg–Witten Floer homology gives invariance under the auxiliary choices.

proof_stages.label: Morse-Bott control in prequantization bundles
proof_stages.summary: For circle bundles over Riemann surfaces, the degenerate fiber Reeb dynamics are perturbed by a Morse function on the base so that low-action generators correspond to fibers over critical points with computable Conley–Zehnder indices and elliptic/hyperbolic types.

proof_stages.label: Exterior-algebra computation of prequantization ECH
proof_stages.summary: The prequantization calculation identifies ECH, after filtered limits, with the exterior algebra of the homology of the base because the relevant ECH differential counts only cylinders projecting to Morse flow lines.

proof_stages.label: Knot-filtered ECH and quantitative Reeb bounds
proof_stages.summary: The final part applies the preceding ECH technology to torus-knot open books, computing knot-filtered ECH through open-book/Seifert/orbibundle presentations and using it with the ECH Weyl law to force Reeb currents satisfying action and linking bounds.
